title: Chemistry Teacher Heuristic Optimization Algorithm
author: Zhang, Jincheng
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This paper proposes a global optimization algorithm inspired by the professional characteristics of chemistry teachers. Drawing on the thinking of chemistry teachers in experimental design and teaching, the algorithm constructs five unique mechanisms: catalyst mechanisms, reagent combination crossover, experimental failure feedback, reaction equilibrium weighting, and knowledge fusion experiments. By combining these mechanisms with mathematical modeling, efficient exploration of the global search space and localized fine-grained optimization are achieved. This paper presents a mathematical description and iterative process of the algorithm, providing a formulated solution space update strategy, offering new ideas and methods for complex optimization problems .
steps_raw: This paper proposes a global optimization algorithm inspired by the professional characteristics of chemistry teachers. Drawing on the thinking of chemistry teachers in experimental design and teaching, the algorithm constructs five unique mechanisms: catalyst mechanisms, reagent combination crossover, experimental failure feedback, reaction equilibrium weighting, and knowledge fusion experiments. By combining these mechanisms with mathematical modeling, efficient exploration of the global search space and localized fine-grained optimization are achieved. This paper presents a mathematical description and iterative process of the algorithm, providing a formulated solution space update strategy, offering new ideas and methods for complex optimization problems .
